title: Sebastiania klotzschiana
taxonomy: Sebastiania klotzschiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Sebastiania, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as the spurge family. The plant is native to South America, specifically in countries such as Brazil and Argentina. Sebastiania klotzschiana is a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and relationships with other plants in its family and order.

intro: Sebastiania klotzschiana, also known as Klotzsch's Sebastiania, is a medicinal plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
